rationale: The citing paper uses Avnet and Higgins (2006) in its theory development as direct evidence that regulatory fit can increase consumer value responses (e.g., willingness to pay). That prior result is used to motivate the paper's core predictions that regulatory compatibility in promotion framing and time restrictions will affect downstream shopping behavior. Although cited with other regulatory-fit papers, it plays a clear supporting role in framing the mechanism and expected direction of effects, not just a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing study treats the cited paper as part of the regulatory-fit value foundation, using it to justify predictions that fit-compatible promotion cues should increase consumer response intensity. It then translates that logic to a new retail-basket context (basket size and composition, including unpromoted items).
